message_coalesce_window_ms.description:
Message coalescing window in milliseconds for Telegram-specific ergonomics (#4145). When set, messages from the same sender arriving within this window are buffered and dispatched to the agent as a single batched context. This prevents out-of-order processing in the common pattern where a user forwards a message and immediately follows up with a comment.

This is a thin alias for [`ChannelOverrides::message_debounce_ms`]: if `overrides.message_debounce_ms` is non-zero it wins; otherwise the value here is applied. `Some(0)` explicitly disables coalescing. `None` (default) leaves behavior unchanged.

thread_routes.description:
Thread-based agent routing for forum topics.

Maps Telegram `message_thread_id` (as string) to an agent name. Messages in a matched thread are routed to that agent instead of the `default_agent`. Unmatched threads fall back to normal routing.

```toml [channels.telegram.thread_routes] "12345" = "research-agent" "67890" = "coding-agent" ```

ParallelToolsConfig.description:
Configuration for the agent loop's parallel tool dispatcher.

PR-3 ships the schema only; the agent loop still runs tool calls strictly sequentially. PR-4 wires the dispatcher into the runtime and PR-5 flips `enabled` on by default.

```toml [parallel_tools] enabled = false max_concurrent = 4 mcp_default_safety = "write_shared"   # or "read_only" mcp_readonly_allowlist = ["mcp__github__list_issues"] ```

Falls back to fully sequential execution when `enabled = false`.

enabled.description: Master switch. Default `false` so PR-3 ships with no behaviour change. PR-5 will flip the default to `true` after the streaming dispatcher integration lands.

max_concurrent.description: Cap on concurrent tool calls within a single bucket. `0` = uncapped (use the bucket size). The dispatcher honours this when launching futures via `join_all`.

mcp_default_safety.description: Default `ParallelSafety` class assigned to MCP tools whose servers don't carry `readOnlyHint` annotations. Conservative default `"write_shared"` keeps unannotated MCP tools serialised (one per bucket) instead of optimistically parallelising. Accepted values: `"read_only"` | `"write_shared"`. PR-4 will promote this to a typed enum once the dispatcher consumes it.

mcp_readonly_allowlist.description: Explicit allowlist of MCP tool names that should be treated as `ReadOnly` regardless of `mcp_default_safety`. Names match the fully-namespaced form (`mcp__server__name`).

api_key_env.description: Env var name holding the API key.

```toml [[context_engine.plugin_registries]] name = "Official" github_repo = "librefang/librefang-registry"

[[context_engine.plugin_registries]] name = "My Company" github_repo = "acme-corp/librefang-plugins" ```

name.description: Human-readable label shown in the dashboard.

PluginsConfig.description:
Plugin registry configuration.

Configure in config.toml: ```toml [plugins] plugin_registries = ["librefang/plugin-registry"] ```

plugin_registries.description: Additional GitHub `owner/repo` plugin registries to search. Merged with `context_engine.plugin_registries`.

PrefixStyle.description:
Prefix style applied to outbound agent messages on a channel.

When enabled, the channel bridge wraps the responding agent's reply with its name so end-users can tell which agent authored the message when multiple agents share the same channel. Default is `Off` to preserve existing behavior.

Platform-native identity (e.g. Slack per-message bot username override, Discord embed author field) is intentionally out of scope here.

oneOf.description: No prefix — byte-identical to pre-feature behavior.
Bold bracketed name via markdown: `**[agent-name]** text`. Renders bold on platforms that support markdown (Discord, Telegram markdown mode, Slack mrkdwn treats it as bold too).

PrivacyConfig.description:
PII privacy controls for LLM context.

When enabled, the runtime filters personally identifiable information (emails, phone numbers, credit card numbers, SSNs) from user messages and sender context before they are sent to LLM providers.

Configure in config.toml: ```toml [privacy] mode = "pseudonymize"  # off | redact | pseudonymize redact_patterns = ["\\b(CUSTOM_ID_\\d+)\\b"] ```

mode.description: Privacy mode: off, redact, or pseudonymize.

redact_patterns.description: Additional regex patterns to match and redact/pseudonymize. These are applied in addition to the built-in PII patterns.

PrivacyMode.description:
PII privacy mode for LLM context filtering.

Controls how personally identifiable information is handled before messages are sent to LLM providers.

oneOf.description: No PII filtering — messages are sent as-is.
Replace detected PII with stable pseudonyms (User-A, User-B, etc.). Pseudonym mappings are stable within a session.

ProactiveMemoryConfig.description:
Configuration for proactive memory system.

Example in config.toml: ```toml [proactive_memory] auto_memorize = true auto_retrieve = true max_retrieve = 10 session_ttl_hours = 24 extraction_model = "gpt-4o-mini"  # optional, enables LLM-powered extraction ```

confidence_decay_rate.description: Confidence decay rate per day. Memories lose confidence over time when not accessed, following exponential decay: `conf * e^(-rate * days)`. Default: 0.01 (very slow — takes ~70 days to halve).

duplicate_threshold.description: Similarity threshold for duplicate detection (0.0 - 1.0). When stored embeddings are available, uses vector cosine similarity (mem0-quality); otherwise falls back to Jaccard word overlap. Default: 0.5.

enabled.description: Master toggle — when false, the entire proactive memory subsystem is disabled.

extraction_model.description: LLM model to use for extraction. If None, uses rule-based extraction.

max_memories_per_agent.description: Maximum number of memories allowed per agent. When adding new memories would exceed this cap, the oldest/lowest-confidence memories are evicted first. Default: 1000. Set to 0 to disable the cap.

max_retrieve.description: Maximum memories to retrieve per query.

session_ttl_hours.description: Session memory TTL in hours. Memories older than this are cleaned up automatically before each agent execution. Default: 24 hours.

PromptIntelligenceConfig.description: Configuration for prompt versioning and A/B testing.

enabled.description: Enable prompt versioning and A/B testing. Default: false.

hash_prompts.description: Hash prompts using SHA-256 for version identification. Default: true.

max_versions_per_agent.description: Maximum number of versions to keep per agent. Default: 50.

ProviderBudget.description:
Per-provider spending limits.

Lets you cap spend on paid providers (e.g. Moonshot, OpenAI) without throttling free local providers (e.g. litellm, ollama). All limits default to 0 which means "unlimited" — only non-zero limits are enforced. Keyed by the provider id in `BudgetConfig.providers`, which must match the `model.provider` field of the agent's `ModelConfig`.

max_cost_per_day_usd.description: Maximum cost in USD per day for this provider (0.0 = unlimited).

max_cost_per_hour_usd.description: Maximum cost in USD per hour for this provider (0.0 = unlimited).

max_cost_per_month_usd.description: Maximum cost in USD per month for this provider (0.0 = unlimited).

max_tokens_per_hour.description: Maximum total tokens per hour for this provider (0 = unlimited).

ProxyConfig.description:
HTTP proxy configuration.

Configure in config.toml: ```toml [proxy] http_proxy = "http://proxy.corp.example:8080" https_proxy = "http://proxy.corp.example:8080" no_proxy = "localhost,127.0.0.1,.internal.corp" ```

Environment variables `HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY` / `NO_PROXY` are also respected as fallbacks when the config fields are empty.

http_proxy.description: HTTP proxy URL (e.g. `http://proxy:8080`). Falls back to `HTTP_PROXY` / `http_proxy` env var.

https_proxy.description: HTTPS proxy URL (e.g. `http://proxy:8080`). Falls back to `HTTPS_PROXY` / `https_proxy` env var.

no_proxy.description: Comma-separated list of hosts/domains that should bypass the proxy. Falls back to `NO_PROXY` / `no_proxy` env var.

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

bot_token_env.description: Env var name holding the bot token.

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

QqConfig.description: QQ Bot API v2 channel adapter configuration.

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

allowed_users.description: QQ user IDs allowed to interact (empty = allow all).

app_secret_env.description: Env var name holding the app secret (NOT the secret itself).

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

QueueConcurrencyConfig.description:
Per-lane concurrency limits for the command queue.

Configure in config.toml: ```toml [queue.concurrency] main_lane = 3 cron_lane = 2 subagent_lane = 3 trigger_lane = 8 default_per_agent = 1 ```

default_per_agent.description: Default per-agent invocation cap when an agent's manifest does not set `max_concurrent_invocations`. `1` reproduces the legacy per-agent-mutex serialization that pre-existed this knob — change deliberately. `0` is rewritten to `1` by validation. Typed `usize` to match the sibling lane fields and to feed `Semaphore::new` without a cast.

trigger_fire_timeout_secs.description: Per-fire timeout (seconds) for trigger dispatches. Bounds the duration a single fire holds its `Lane::Trigger` and per-agent permits, preventing one stuck LLM call from starving every other agent's triggers kernel-wide (issue #3446). `0` is rewritten to the default by validation.

trigger_lane.description: Trigger lane concurrent limit — global cap on event-trigger (`TaskPosted`, `MessageReceived`, …) dispatches in flight at the same time, across all agents. Acquired BEFORE the per-agent semaphore so a single hot agent cannot starve the kernel. Default `8`. `0` is rewritten to `1` by validation.

QueueConfig.description:
Message queue configuration.

Controls queue depth limits and task TTL for the agent command queue.

Configure in config.toml: ```toml [queue] max_depth_per_agent = 100 max_depth_global = 1000 task_ttl_secs = 3600 ```

max_depth_per_agent.description: Max queue depth per agent (0 = unlimited).

task_queue_retention_days.description: How many days to keep `completed` / `failed` / `cancelled` rows in `task_queue` before the periodic retention sweep hard-deletes them. Default: 7. Set to 0 to disable pruning (queue grows forever — #3466).

task_ttl_secs.description: Task TTL in seconds (unprocessed tasks expire, 0 = unlimited).

RateLimitConfig.description:
API and WebSocket rate limiting configuration.

Controls GCRA token-bucket rate limiting for HTTP API requests and per-connection limits for WebSocket connections.

api_requests_per_minute.description: API token budget per minute per IP (GCRA algorithm). Default: 500.

auth_rate_limit_per_ip.description: Max login attempts per IP per 15-minute window on auth endpoints (`/api/auth/dashboard-login`, `/api/auth/login*`). Default: 10. Set to 0 to disable the per-IP auth rate limiter.

max_ws_per_ip.description: Maximum concurrent WebSocket connections per IP. Default: 5.

retry_after_secs.description: Retry-After header value in seconds when rate limited. Default: 60.

ws_debounce_chars.description: Flush text buffer when it exceeds this many characters. Default: 200.

ws_debounce_ms.description: Text delta debounce interval in milliseconds. Default: 100.

ws_idle_timeout_secs.description: WebSocket idle timeout in seconds (close after inactivity). Default: 1800.

ws_messages_per_minute.description: Maximum WebSocket messages per minute per connection. Default: 10.

ws_terminal_messages_per_minute.description:
Maximum terminal WebSocket input messages per minute per connection. Default: 3600.

Terminal sessions send one WebSocket message per keystroke, so the generic `ws_messages_per_minute = 10` (sized for chat WS where a "message" is a whole utterance) is two orders of magnitude too low for an interactive PTY — typing `vim` + `:wq` in vim already exhausts the budget and the session appears to freeze. 3600/min (60/sec ≈ 720 WPM) covers any human typing speed plus TUI navigation bursts while still capping pathological floods.

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

client_secret_env.description: Env var name holding the client secret.

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

password_env.description: Env var name holding the bot password.

RegistryConfig.description:
Registry sync configuration.

Configure in config.toml: ```toml [registry] cache_ttl_secs = 86400 # Optional: proxy/mirror prefix for users behind the GFW. # All GitHub URLs are prefixed with this value, e.g. #   registry_mirror = "https://ghproxy.cn" # turns "https://github.com/..." into "https://ghproxy.cn/https://github.com/..." registry_mirror = "" # Optional: full base URL of the registry repo (without trailing slash). # Replaces the built-in librefang/librefang-registry default entirely. # Empty = use upstream default. base_url = "https://github.com/GQAdonis/librefang-registry" ```

base_url.description:
Full base URL of the registry repository (no trailing slash). When non-empty, replaces the built-in upstream default (`https://github.com/librefang/librefang-registry`). The tarball URL is derived as `{base_url}/archive/refs/heads/main.tar.gz` and the git clone URL as `{base_url}.git`. The tarball prefix (`{repo-name}-main/`) is derived from the URL's last path segment.

Empty string = use the upstream default (for fallback / emergency rollback without recompiling).

cache_ttl_secs.description: Cache TTL for registry sync in seconds (default: 86400 = 24 hours). The registry is re-downloaded when the local cache is older than this.

registry_mirror.description:
Mirror/proxy prefix for GitHub URLs. When non-empty, all outbound GitHub requests (tarball downloads, git clones, raw content fetches) are prefixed with this value. Useful for users in China Mainland where direct GitHub access is slow or blocked.

Example: `"https://ghproxy.cn"` rewrites `https://github.com/...` → `https://ghproxy.cn/https://github.com/...`

ReloadConfig.description: Configuration for config file watching and hot-reload.

oneOf.description: Hot-reload safe sections only (channels, skills, heartbeat).
Hot-reload where possible, flag restart-required otherwise.

RemoteSurrealConfig.description:
Connection details for a remote SurrealDB 3.0 instance.

The same struct is reused by [`crate::config::StorageConfig`] (for librefang's own session) and by `librefang_types::config::UarConfig` (when UAR is configured to reuse the same instance).

password_env.description: Name of the environment variable (or `vault:` reference) that holds the password. We never persist the password itself in config files.

tls_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification. **Never** enable in production.

username.description: Username for namespace- or database-level authentication.

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

allowed_channels.description: Restrict to specific channel IDs (empty = all channels the bot is in).

api_url.description: Revolt API URL (set to your self-hosted instance URL if not using revolt.chat).

bot_token_env.description: Env var name holding the bot token.

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

ws_url.description: Revolt WebSocket URL (set to your self-hosted instance WS URL if not using revolt.chat).

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

token_env.description: Env var name holding the auth token.

SanitizeConfig.description:
Configuration for channel input sanitization / prompt-injection detection.

The sanitizer is **enabled by default** (mode = "block"). To opt out set `disable_input_sanitizer = true` in `[sanitize]` or change `mode`:

```toml [sanitize] mode = "block"          # off | warn | block  (default: block) max_message_length = 32768 custom_block_patterns = ["(?i)secret\\s+code"] # disable_input_sanitizer = true  # emergency opt-out ```

custom_block_patterns.description: Additional regex patterns that should trigger a block/warn.

disable_input_sanitizer.description: Emergency opt-out: set to `true` to disable all input sanitization. Not recommended for production — prefer `mode = "warn"` for monitoring without blocking, or `mode = "off"` for a softer disable.

max_message_length.description: Maximum allowed message length in bytes (default: 32 768).

SanitizeMode.description: Input sanitization mode for channel messages.

oneOf.description: No checking — all messages pass through. Set `mode = "off"` in `[sanitize]` to opt out of prompt-injection detection.
Log a warning but allow the message through.
Reject the message and send an error to the user (default).
SearXNG self-hosted search (no API key needed).
Auto-select based on available API keys (Tavily → Brave → Jina → Perplexity → Searxng → DuckDuckGo).

SearxngSearchConfig.description:
SearXNG self-hosted search configuration.

Requires only a `url`; SearXNG public instances reject `limit` and the LLM-facing `max_results` is taken from the per-call `tool_args` (the runtime truncates client-side after fetching).

url.description: Base URL of the SearXNG instance (e.g., "https://search.example.com"). Empty means the provider is disabled.

SecondFactor.description:
Second-factor verification scope.

Controls where TOTP verification is enforced: - `Totp` = approvals only (backward-compatible default when TOTP is enabled) - `Login` = dashboard login only - `Both` = approvals + dashboard login

oneOf.description: TOTP required for tool approvals only.
TOTP required for dashboard login only.
TOTP required for both approvals and dashboard login.

SessionConfig.description:
Session retention policy configuration.

Controls automatic cleanup of idle or excess sessions and optional startup prompt injection. Configure in `config.toml`: ```toml [session] retention_days = 30 max_sessions_per_agent = 100 cleanup_interval_hours = 24 reset_prompt = "You are a helpful coding assistant. Always respond in English." ```

cleanup_interval_hours.description: How often the cleanup job runs (in hours).

context_injection.description: Context injections applied to every new or reset session. Each entry specifies content, a positional slot, and an optional condition.

max_sessions_per_agent.description: Maximum number of sessions per agent (oldest pruned first, 0 = unlimited).

on_session_start_script.description: Optional shell script to run when a new session is created (fire-and-forget).

reset.description: Automatic session-reset policy (idle timeout and/or daily fixed-time reset). Default: `mode = "off"` — no automatic resets, fully backward-compatible.

reset_prompt.description: Optional message injected as the first system message when a new session starts or when the session is reset. Useful for setting up persistent context or instructions across all agents.

retention_days.description: Maximum age for idle sessions before automatic cleanup (days, 0 = unlimited).

SessionResetMode.description: Which automatic-reset strategy is active for a session.

oneOf.description: No automatic reset. Sessions persist indefinitely. (default)
Reset once per day at `daily_at_hour` (local clock, 0-23).
Reset when *either* idle or daily condition is satisfied.

SessionResetPolicy.description:
Per-session auto-reset policy.

Configured inside `[session.reset]` in `config.toml`: ```toml [session.reset] mode = "idle" idle_minutes = 1440   # 24 h

# or mode = "both" idle_minutes = 60 daily_at_hour = 4 ```

daily_at_hour.description: Hour of day (0–23, local clock) at which the `Daily` / `Both` reset fires. Default: 4 (04:00 local).

idle_minutes.description: Inactivity threshold in minutes for `Idle` / `Both` modes. Default: 1440 (24 hours).

mode.description: Which reset strategy (or strategies) to apply.

SidecarChannelConfig.description:
Configuration for a sidecar channel adapter (external process-based).

Sidecar adapters allow external processes written in any language to act as channel adapters. Communication uses newline-delimited JSON over stdin/stdout.

Configure in config.toml: ```toml [[sidecar_channels]] name = "my-telegram" command = "python3" args = ["adapters/telegram_adapter.py"] env = { TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN = "xxx" } ```

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command.

env.description: Extra environment variables to pass to the subprocess.

SignalConfig.description: Signal channel adapter configuration (via signal-cli REST API).

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

allow_local.description: When `true`, allow the `api_url` to point at loopback / RFC-1918 addresses. Defaults to `false`; set to `true` only when signal-cli runs on localhost.

api_key.description: Optional API key sent as `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` on every request. If absent, requests are sent without an Authorization header.

api_url.description: URL of the signal-cli REST API (e.g., "http://localhost:8080").

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

poll_interval_secs.description: Poll interval in seconds for checking new messages (default: 2).

disabled.description: Names of skills to skip at load time. Useful for quickly disabling a skill (agent-evolved or marketplace-installed) without deleting its directory. Matching is case-sensitive on the skill manifest name.

env_passthrough_denied_patterns.description: Operator-side gate over skill `env_passthrough` requests: glob patterns that block matching env-var names regardless of what the skill manifest declares. Defaults to a deny list covering common credential conventions (`*_KEY`, `*_TOKEN`, `*_PASSWORD`, `*_SECRET`, `*_API_KEY`, `AWS_*`, `GITHUB_*`). Set to an empty list to disable the operator deny check; the built-in `FORBIDDEN_PASSTHROUGH` and kernel-reserved hard blocks still apply.

env_passthrough_per_skill.description:
Per-skill explicit allow overrides. Lets the operator grant a specific skill an env var that would otherwise be blocked by `env_passthrough_denied_patterns`. Cannot bypass the built-in `FORBIDDEN_PASSTHROUGH` hard block.

Example: `{ "gog" = ["GOG_KEYRING_PASSWORD"] }`.

extra_dirs.description: Extra skill directories to scan in addition to `~/.librefang/skills/`. Each entry must be an absolute path. Scanned read-only after the primary skills dir; local skills with the same name win.

load_user.description: Whether user-installed skills from the skills directory are loaded. Default: true.

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

allowed_channels.description: Channel IDs allowed to interact (empty = allow all).

app_token_env.description: Env var name holding the app-level token (xapp-) for Socket Mode.

bot_token_env.description: Env var name holding the bot token (xoxb-) for REST API.

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

force_flat_replies.description: When true, bot replies are posted as top-level channel messages instead of threaded replies. Defaults to `None` (i.e. use normal threading).

initial_backoff_secs.description: Initial backoff in seconds on WebSocket failures (default: 1).

max_backoff_secs.description: Maximum backoff in seconds on WebSocket failures (default: 60).

unfurl_links.description: Whether to disable link unfurling (preview expansion) in sent messages. When set to `false`, Slack will not expand link previews. When `None` (default), Slack uses its own default behavior.

SlackRoleMapping.description: Slack-side mapping. Slack's `users.info` exposes `is_owner` / `is_admin` / `is_restricted` / `is_ultra_restricted`. Precedence (owner > admin > guest > member) is collapsed inside the channel adapter (`SlackAdapter::parse_users_info_response`) into a single platform token before this mapping ever sees it; the translator here is a flat lookup, not a precedence ladder. Each step is optional — leave a field unset to fall through to default-deny `Viewer` for that platform tier.

member_role.description: LibreFang role for regular workspace members.

host.description: Hostname or IP of the remote machine.

host_key_sha256.description: SHA-256 hex of the expected server host key. When set, the backend refuses to connect if the server presents a different key (TOFU-style pinning). When empty, the backend falls back to the on-disk known-hosts file at `~/.librefang/ssh_known_hosts.toml` — first connect records the fingerprint there, subsequent connects must match.

key_passphrase_env.description: Environment variable holding the passphrase for an encrypted `key_path`. When unset, `key_path` is loaded as if unencrypted — matching the historical behaviour. Setting this lets operators store an encrypted key on disk without passing the passphrase in plaintext through the daemon config.

key_path.description: Path to a private key on disk. Mutually exclusive with `password_env`.

password_env.description: Environment variable holding the SSH password. Falls back to passwordless keyless auth if neither this nor `key_path` is set (useful only for hosts with `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` already holding the daemon's key).

timeout_secs.description: Optional command timeout in seconds. Default: 60.

user.description: Login user. Default: empty (must be set when backend is active).

workdir.description: Optional working directory on the remote host. Empty = remote `$HOME`.

StorageBackendKind.description:
Where the storage backend lives.

`Embedded` uses the bundled RocksDB engine and is the default chosen by the wizard. `Remote` connects to an external SurrealDB 3.0 instance and can be shared with the Universal Agent Runtime via separate namespaces.

oneOf.description: Single-process embedded SurrealDB. Cannot be shared between processes — see surreal-memory-server's CLAUDE.md.

path.description: Filesystem path of the RocksDB directory.

oneOf.description: Remote SurrealDB 3.0 instance reachable over WebSocket or HTTP.

password_env.description: Name of the environment variable (or `vault:` reference) that holds the password. We never persist the password itself in config files.

tls_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification. **Never** enable in production.

username.description: Username for namespace- or database-level authentication.

StorageConfig.description:
Top-level storage configuration on `KernelConfig`.

Defaults to embedded SurrealDB at `<data_dir>/librefang.surreal`.

backend.description: Which backend to use at startup.

legacy_sqlite_path.description: Optional path to a legacy `librefang.db` SQLite file. When set, the `storage migrate` CLI / `POST /api/storage/migrate` endpoint can stream rows from it into the active SurrealDB store.

namespace.description: Default namespace when [`Self::backend`] is `Embedded`. For `Remote` the namespace inside the [`RemoteSurrealConfig`] takes precedence.

TaintRuleId.description:
Identifies a specific taint detection rule.

Used by [`check_outbound_text_violation_with_skip`] to let callers opt out of individual rules without touching the main code path.  Per-tool, per-path exemptions in `McpTaintPolicy` map directly to these variants.

oneOf.description: Blocks `key=value` / `key: value` / `"key":` shapes for known secret-key names.
Blocks long opaque tokens (≥32 mixed-alnum chars without a date component).
Blocks JSON object keys whose name is a well-known credential key (e.g. `authorization`, `api_key`, `secret`).

TaskBoardConfig.description:
Task-board (shared-task-queue) safety knobs.

When a worker agent calls `task_claim` the task transitions to `in_progress` and `claimed_at` is stamped. If the worker's LLM stalls (empty response, crash, timeout) it will never call `task_complete` and the task would otherwise stay `in_progress` forever — no retry, no external signal, no way for the delegator to know something broke (issues #2923, #2926).

The sweeper runs in the kernel and flips stuck tasks back to `pending` so they can be reclaimed, clearing `assigned_to` in the process.

Configure in config.toml: ```toml [task_board] claim_ttl_secs = 600         # 10 minutes — auto-reset after this sweep_interval_secs = 30     # how often the sweeper runs ```

Setting `claim_ttl_secs = 0` disables the sweeper entirely — useful for long-running human-in-the-loop tasks where a 10 minute reset would be wrong.

claim_ttl_secs.description: How long an `in_progress` task may stay claimed before the sweeper resets it to `pending`. Default: 600 s (10 minutes). 0 disables.

max_retries.description: Maximum number of auto-resets before a stuck task is marked `failed`. Default: 0 = no limit (retry indefinitely).

sweep_interval_secs.description: How often the sweeper scans for stuck tasks. Default: 30 s.

api_key_env.description: Env var name holding the API key.

TeamsConfig.description: Microsoft Teams (Bot Framework v3) channel adapter configuration.

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

app_password_env.description: Env var name holding the app password.

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

security_token_env.description: Env var name holding the outgoing webhook security token (base64-encoded). Used for HMAC-SHA256 verification of inbound webhook requests. Required by default; setting `signature_required = false` opts out (dev only).

signature_required.description: Reject adapter startup unless a security token is configured (default `true`). Setting to `false` is strongly discouraged — webhook becomes a public endpoint.

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

allowed_users.description: Telegram user IDs allowed to interact (empty = allow all). Accepts strings for consistency; numeric TOML integers are coerced to strings.

api_url.description: Custom Telegram Bot API base URL for proxies or mirrors. Defaults to `https://api.telegram.org` when not set.

bot_token_env.description: Env var name holding the bot token (NOT the token itself).

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

initial_backoff_secs.description: Initial backoff in seconds on API failures (default: 1).

long_poll_timeout_secs.description: Long-poll timeout in seconds sent to getUpdates (default: 30).

max_backoff_secs.description: Maximum backoff in seconds on API failures (default: 60).

message_coalesce_window_ms.description:
Message coalescing window in milliseconds for Telegram-specific ergonomics (#4145). When set, messages from the same sender arriving within this window are buffered and dispatched to the agent as a single batched context. This prevents out-of-order processing in the common pattern where a user forwards a message and immediately follows up with a comment.

This is a thin alias for [`ChannelOverrides::message_debounce_ms`]: if `overrides.message_debounce_ms` is non-zero it wins; otherwise the value here is applied. `Some(0)` explicitly disables coalescing. `None` (default) leaves behavior unchanged.

thread_routes.description:
Thread-based agent routing for forum topics.

Maps Telegram `message_thread_id` (as string) to an agent name. Messages in a matched thread are routed to that agent instead of the `default_agent`. Unmatched threads fall back to normal routing.

```toml [channels.telegram.thread_routes] "12345" = "research-agent" "67890" = "coding-agent" ```

TelegramRoleMapping.description: Telegram-side mapping. Telegram exposes three statuses for a member of a chat: `creator`, `administrator`, `member` (plus `restricted`/`left`/ `kicked` which we collapse into "no derived role").

admin_role.description: LibreFang role assigned when Telegram reports `status = "administrator"`.

creator_role.description: LibreFang role assigned when Telegram reports `status = "creator"`.

member_role.description: LibreFang role assigned when Telegram reports `status = "member"`.

TelemetryConfig.description:
Telemetry / observability configuration.

```toml [telemetry] enabled = true                              # OpenTelemetry OTLP tracing otlp_endpoint = "http://localhost:4317" service_name = "librefang" sample_rate = 1.0 prometheus_enabled = true                   # Prometheus metrics at /api/metrics ```

auto_start_observability_stack.description:
Auto-start the bundled observability Docker stack (Grafana, Prometheus, Tempo, OTel collector) on daemon boot. Default: `false`.

Off by default because spinning up four containers on every `librefang start` is a strong implicit side-effect — operators usually prefer `librefang start` to leave the host untouched. Existing dashboards / custom OTel collectors keep working as long as `otlp_endpoint` points at them; the stack is only useful for the bundled local view.

Issue #3136.

emit_caller_trace_headers.description:
Emit `x-librefang-{agent,session,step}-id` HTTP headers on outbound OpenAI-compatible LLM requests so observability sidecars (logging gateways, audit proxies, OTel collectors that shape spans from request metadata) can correlate request log records to the originating agent / session / agent-loop iteration without parsing the JSON body. Default: `true`.

Set to `false` to suppress all three headers wire-side regardless of whether the kernel populated `CompletionRequest`'s caller-id fields. Useful for operators with strict zero-egress policies (regulated tenants, EU healthcare) who want no LibreFang-internal identifiers crossing the upstream-provider boundary, even though the IDs are opaque UUIDs / integers and carry no PII.

Currently consulted only by the OpenAI-compatible driver. Other drivers (Anthropic, Gemini, Bedrock, Vertex, ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Qwen Code) do not emit these headers today; when they grow per-driver header-emission support, they will honour the same flag.

enabled.description: Enable OpenTelemetry OTLP tracing export.

sample_rate.description: Trace sampling rate (0.0 to 1.0). Default: 1.0 (sample everything).

service_name.description: Service name reported to the OTel collector.

TerminalConfig.description:
Terminal / CLI access control configuration.

Controls which clients may connect to the interactive terminal (WebSocket) and how locality is determined.

allow_remote.description: Allow terminal access from remote/proxied connections when no auth is configured. Default: false (local-only when unauthenticated).

allow_unauthenticated_remote.description: Hard-override for the "remote + no authentication" combination. When `allow_remote` is true and no auth is configured, the terminal will still refuse every connection unless this flag is explicitly set to `true`. Intended as a foot-gun guard: enabling `allow_remote` alone is not enough to expose an unauthenticated shell to the network. Default: false.

allowed_origins.description: Additional allowed WebSocket origins beyond auto-detected localhost. Use when the dashboard is served from a custom domain (e.g. "https://my.domain.com").

enabled.description: Master switch — set to false to disable the terminal entirely.

max_windows.description: Maximum number of tmux windows that may exist simultaneously. Guards against DoS.

require_proxy_headers.description:
When true, bare-loopback connections (127.0.0.1 / ::1 with no proxy headers) are rejected at auth time — only connections that arrived via a reverse proxy (carrying X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP) are considered "local". Enable only when running behind a reverse proxy that strips direct loopback access. Default: false.

(Historically named `trust_proxy_headers`; the old name is still accepted for backward compatibility via `serde(alias)`.)

tmux_binary_path.description: Optional explicit path to the `tmux` binary. If None, resolve via PATH.

tmux_enabled.description: Enable tmux-backed multi-window terminal. Only effective when `tmux` binary is available.

stream_thinking.description: Whether to stream thinking tokens to the client.

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

secret_env.description: Env var name holding the API secret.

TimeoutFallback.description: Behavior when an approval request times out.

oneOf.description: Skip the tool — agent continues without executing it.
Extend timeout and re-notify. `extra_timeout_secs` is added each escalation.

ToolExecConfig.description:
Top-level tool-execution backend configuration in `config.toml`.

```toml [tool_exec] kind = "local"   # default

# NOTE: setting kind != "local" here selects the resolved backend at # boot, but actual tool dispatch through the new ToolExecBackend # trait is wired up in a follow-up PR. Until then, tool calls fall # back to the legacy local/docker path. See # docs/architecture/tool-exec-backends.md "Status" section.

# [tool_exec.ssh] # host = "build.example.com" # user = "agent" # key_path = "/home/me/.ssh/id_ed25519"

# [tool_exec.daytona] # api_url = "https://app.daytona.io" # api_key_env = "DAYTONA_API_KEY" ```

`kind` selects the active backend; the matching sub-table carries the connection knobs. Inactive sub-tables are ignored — keeping stale config around is fine.

ToolGroup.description: Tool group — named collection of tool patterns.

tools.description: Tool name patterns in this group.

ToolInvokeConfig.description:
Configuration for `POST /api/tools/{name}/invoke`.

The direct-invoke endpoint bypasses the agent loop, so the usual capability gate (agent manifest `tools` list) does not apply. To avoid a situation where any holder of an API key can call any tool, this endpoint is fail-closed: disabled by default, and — when enabled — only the tools whose names match one of the glob patterns in `allowlist` may be executed.

```toml [tool_invoke] enabled = true allowlist = ["web_search", "web_fetch", "file_read"] ```

Pitfall: `allowlist = ["*"]` matches every tool and effectively turns the endpoint into "give API-key holders the same power as the kernel". Prefer narrow globs (`"file_*"`, `"web_*"`) — reserve `"*"` for trusted single-tenant dev environments.

allowlist.description: Glob patterns of tool names that may be invoked via the REST endpoint (e.g. `"web_*"`, `"file_read"`). Empty list denies all invocations even when `enabled = true`.

enabled.description: Master switch. When `false` (default) the endpoint rejects every request with 403 regardless of the allowlist.

groups.description: Named tool groups for grouping patterns.

ToolPolicyRule.description: A single tool policy rule with glob pattern support.

effect.description: Whether to allow or deny matching tools.

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match tool names (e.g., "shell_*", "web_*", "mcp_github_*").

ToolResultsConfig.description:
Tool-result context budget and artifact spill configuration.

Controls what happens when a tool returns a very large payload.  The primary mechanism shipped in #3347 1/N is **artifact spill**: responses larger than `spill_threshold_bytes` are written to `~/.librefang/data/artifacts/` and the agent receives a compact stub with a handle it can pass to `read_artifact` to retrieve the content in chunks.

`max_bytes_per_turn` enforces a per-turn cumulative byte cap (#3347 2/N). `history_fold_after_turns` triggers tool-result history summarisation via the aux-LLM channel (#3347 3/N) — falls back to byte truncation when no aux-LLM is configured. `artifact_max_age_days` evicts stale spill artifacts at daemon startup (#3347 4/N).  Set to `0` to disable eviction entirely.

```toml [tool_results] spill_threshold_bytes    = 16384        # 16 KB — spill to artifact store above this max_artifact_bytes       = 67108864     # 64 MiB — per-artifact write cap max_bytes_per_turn       = 50000        # cumulative byte cap across all tool results in one turn history_fold_after_turns = 8            # fold stale tool results after this many turns artifact_max_age_days    = 30           # evict spill artifacts older than this on startup; 0 disables ```

artifact_max_age_days.description: Evict spill artifacts older than this many days at daemon startup (#3347 4/N).  The artifact store grows unbounded otherwise — every large tool result writes a content-addressed file under `~/.librefang/data/artifacts/` and the original `read_artifact` handle in the message history is the only thing pinning it.  After history compaction or a long agent lifetime those handles are no longer reachable, but the bytes remain on disk.  GC runs once per daemon boot, fire-and-forget. Set to `0` to disable eviction entirely.  Default: 30 days.

fold_min_batch_size.description: Minimum number of newly-stale tool-result messages required to trigger a fold pass.  Without a batch threshold a long-running session would drag exactly one new message across the staleness boundary every turn and pay an aux-LLM round-trip per turn just to fold a single message.  Skipping until at least N have accumulated amortises that cost.  Set to `1` to fold every turn (no batching); `0` is treated as `1`.  Default: 4.

history_fold_after_turns.description: Fold (summarise via aux-LLM) stale tool results after this many turns (#3347 3/N).  Tool-result messages older than this threshold have each `ContentBlock::ToolResult.content` rewritten in place to a compact `[history-fold] <summary>` stub before the next LLM call. `tool_use_id` / `tool_name` / `is_error` / `status` are preserved so every assistant `tool_use` block keeps its matching `tool_result` (provider APIs reject mismatched ids with 400). Falls back to a static `[summarisation unavailable]` stub when no aux-LLM is configured or the aux call fails, so stale payload is always removed from context.  Default: 8 turns.

max_artifact_bytes.description: Maximum bytes for a single artifact write.  Spill is skipped (falling back to truncation) when a tool result exceeds this cap, preventing a single oversized response from filling the artifact store. Default: 67 108 864 bytes (64 MiB).

max_bytes_per_turn.description: Cumulative byte cap across all tool results in a single LLM turn (#3347 2/N).  When the running total would exceed this, the next result is escalated to artifact spill (or tail truncation if spill fails).  Resets between assistant turns.  Default: 50 000 bytes.

spill_threshold_bytes.description: Spill threshold in bytes.  Tool results larger than this are written to the artifact store; the agent receives a stub with a `read_artifact` handle instead of the raw payload.  Default: 16 384 bytes (16 KB).

cooldown_secs.description: Default cooldown between trigger firings in seconds (default: 5).

max_per_event.description: Maximum triggers that can fire per single event (default: 10).

max_workflow_secs.description: Maximum workflow execution time in seconds (default: 3600).

enabled.description: Enable TTS. Default: false.

max_text_length.description: Max text length for TTS (chars). Default: 4096.

timeout_secs.description: Timeout per TTS request in seconds. Default: 30.

voice.description: Voice: alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, shimmer. Default: "alloy".

UarConfig.description:
Configuration for the Universal Agent Runtime (UAR) integration.

All fields are optional; unset values fall back to the corresponding `UAR_LLM__*` / `LLM_*` environment variables that the UAR driver already consults at runtime.

api_key.description:
LLM API key forwarded to the UAR `LiterLlmDriver`.

Takes precedence over `UAR_LLM__API_KEY` and `LLM_API_KEY` env vars. Use `vault:KEY` syntax to store securely: `api_key = "vault:uar_api_key"`.

base_url.description:
Optional base URL override for the UAR LLM backend.

Useful for pointing at a local proxy (e.g. `"http://localhost:4000"`) without changing the model string.  Takes precedence over `UAR_LLM__BASE_URL` and `LLM_BASE_URL` env vars.

model.description:
Default model in `provider/model` format (e.g. `"openai/gpt-4o"`).

Takes precedence over `UAR_LLM__MODEL` and `LLM_MODEL` env vars.

remote.description:
When set, UAR connects to this remote SurrealDB instance instead of the embedded path in [`Self::surreal_data_dir`].

If both fields are set, [`Self::remote`] wins. This is the second half of the shared-remote-instance design from Phase 4b of the `surrealdb-storage-swap` plan.

share_librefang_storage.description: When `true`, UAR reuses the same `RemoteSurrealConfig` as `KernelConfig.storage` (with `namespace = "uar"` so isolation is preserved). Defaults to `false`.

surreal_data_dir.description:
Filesystem path for the embedded SurrealDB (RocksDB) data store.

Defaults to `<kernel.data_dir>/uar-surreal`.  Set an explicit path when you want to place the UAR database on a separate volume.

UpdateChannel.description:
CLI update channel (like Apple software update channels).

Controls which GitHub releases are considered for `librefang update`: - **Stable**: only non-prerelease tags (default). - **Beta**: stable + beta tags (excludes `-rc`). - **Rc**: all tags including release candidates.

UsageFooterMode.description: Controls what usage info appears in response footers.

UserBudgetConfig.description:
RBAC M5: per-user spending budget.

Mirrors the global [`BudgetConfig`] window structure (hourly / daily / monthly) so the same cost-attribution pipeline can enforce both. Set any limit to `0.0` for "unlimited on that window". `alert_threshold` is the fraction of any limit at which the metering layer should emit a `BudgetExceeded` audit pre-warning (default 0.8, clamped to 0..=1).

alert_threshold.description: Alert threshold (0..=1). Metering surfaces a BudgetExceeded audit when *any* window reaches this fraction of its limit. Defaults to 0.8 — same default as the global budget — for consistency.

max_daily_usd.description: Maximum cost in USD per day for this user (0.0 = unlimited).

max_hourly_usd.description: Maximum cost in USD per hour for this user (0.0 = unlimited).

max_monthly_usd.description: Maximum cost in USD per month for this user (0.0 = unlimited).

UserConfig.description: User configuration for RBAC multi-user support.

api_key_hash.description: Optional API key hash for API authentication.

budget.description: RBAC M5: per-user spend caps. `None` means "no per-user cap" — the user is still bounded by global / per-agent / per-provider budgets. See [`UserBudgetConfig`] for the supported windows.

channel_bindings.description: Channel bindings: maps channel platform IDs to this user. e.g., {"telegram": "123456", "discord": "987654"}

channel_tool_rules.description: Per-channel tool overrides for THIS user. Keyed by channel adapter name (e.g. `"telegram"`, `"discord"`). Layers on top of the global `ApprovalPolicy.channel_rules` — both must agree to allow.

memory_access.description: Memory namespace ACL — controls reads/writes to memory scopes (`proactive`, `kv:*`, etc.) and PII redaction. `None` means "use the role default ACL".

tool_categories.description: Bulk allow/deny by `ToolGroup` category (groups are declared in `KernelConfig.tool_policy.groups`). Lets admins say `denied_groups = ["dangerous"]` without listing each tool.

tool_policy.description: Per-user tool allow/deny lists. Layered ON TOP of the per-agent `ToolPolicy` and any channel rules in `ApprovalPolicy`. `None` means "no per-user policy — defer to other layers".

UserMemoryAccess.description:
Per-user memory namespace ACL.

Memory in LibreFang is partitioned by *namespace* — typically the agent ID for KV / proactive entries, plus a small set of well-known shared scopes (`shared`, `proactive`, `kv`, …). This ACL gates which of those a given user may read or write through the LLM-facing memory tools.

PII handling: when `pii_access` is `false`, fragments tagged with [`TaintLabel::Pii`](crate::taint::TaintLabel::Pii) MUST be redacted before they reach the user. The redaction itself happens at the memory call site (kernel + memory crate); this struct only declares intent.

delete_allowed.description: Whether the user may delete memory entries they can otherwise read.

export_allowed.description: Whether the user may export memory in bulk.

pii_access.description: Whether PII-tagged fragments may be returned to this user. When `false`, PII fields are redacted on read.

readable_namespaces.description: Namespaces this user may read. Empty list with `pii_access=false` is a meaningful "no-read" deny-all — see [`Self::can_read`].

writable_namespaces.description: Namespaces this user may write to. Empty list = no-write (read-only).

UserToolCategories.description:
Bulk allow/deny by tool category — references existing `ToolGroup` definitions by name (e.g. `"web_tools"`, `"code_tools"`). Group definitions live in [`KernelConfig.tool_policy.groups`](crate::tool_policy::ToolPolicy::groups).

Categories let admins say "this user only gets read-only categories" without listing every tool individually.

allowed_groups.description: Group names whose tools are allowed for this user. Empty = no category-level allow-list.

denied_groups.description: Group names whose tools are denied for this user. Always wins.

UserToolPolicy.description:
Per-user allow/deny lists for tool invocations.

These rules layer on top of the per-agent [`ToolPolicy`](crate::tool_policy::ToolPolicy) and the per-agent channel rules in [`ApprovalPolicy`](crate::approval::ApprovalPolicy). All layers must agree (fail-closed AND) for a call to proceed without approval.

allowed_tools.description: Tool name patterns this user may invoke. Empty list means "no allow-list — defer to other layers". When non-empty, every invocation must match at least one pattern.

denied_tools.description: Tool name patterns this user must never invoke. Always wins.

enabled.description: Whether the vault is enabled (auto-detected if vault.enc exists).

use_os_keyring.description:
Whether to store the vault master key in the OS keyring (Linux Secret Service / macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager).

`None` = use the platform default. macOS defaults to `false` because the Keychain ACL is per-binary signature: every `cargo build` produces a new signature and triggers a fresh "allow" prompt on daemon restart. Linux and Windows default to `true`.

The env var `LIBREFANG_VAULT_NO_KEYRING=1` overrides this setting and forces the file-based fallback regardless of the config or platform default. The fallback path is resolved via `dirs::data_local_dir()`: - macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/librefang/.keyring` - Linux: `~/.local/share/librefang/.keyring` - Windows: `%LOCALAPPDATA%\librefang\.keyring`

The file is AES-256-GCM-wrapped with an Argon2id-derived machine-fingerprint key and stored mode 0600.

VertexAiConfig.description:
Vertex AI provider configuration.

Configure in config.toml: ```toml [vertex_ai] project_id = "my-gcp-project" region = "us-central1" credentials_path = "/path/to/service-account.json" ```

Credentials resolution order: 1. `credentials_path` in config (JSON string or file path) 2. `VERTEX_AI_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` env var 3. `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` env var (file path) 4. `gcloud auth print-access-token` CLI fallback

credentials_path.description: Path to a GCP service account JSON key file, or the raw JSON string. Falls back to `VERTEX_AI_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` or `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` env var.

project_id.description: GCP project ID. Falls back to `VERTEX_AI_PROJECT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`, or the `project_id` field in the service account JSON.

region.description: GCP region for the Vertex AI endpoint (default: "us-central1"). Falls back to `VERTEX_AI_REGION` or `GOOGLE_CLOUD_REGION` env var.

VoiceConfig.description:
Voice channel adapter configuration.

Runs a WebSocket server that accepts audio streams, transcribes via STT, sends text to the agent, and returns synthesized speech via TTS.

```toml [channels.voice] listen_port = 4546 api_key_env = "OPENAI_API_KEY" stt_url = "https://api.openai.com" tts_url = "https://api.openai.com" tts_voice = "alloy" ```

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this voice instance (used for multi-bot routing).

api_key_env.description: Env var name holding the API key for STT/TTS services.

buffer_threshold.description: Audio buffer threshold in bytes before triggering STT (default: 32768).

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route voice messages to.

stt_url.description: Base URL for the STT (Speech-to-Text) API.

tts_url.description: Base URL for the TTS (Text-to-Speech) API.

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs (empty = allow all). Format: `{hash}@im.wechat`.

bot_token_env.description: Env var name holding the bot token from a previous QR login session. If the env var is set and non-empty, the adapter skips QR login.

initial_backoff_secs.description: Initial backoff in seconds on API failures (default: 2).

max_backoff_secs.description: Maximum backoff in seconds on API failures (default: 60).

WeComConfig.description:
WeCom intelligent bot adapter configuration.

Supports two connection modes: - `websocket` (default): connects to `wss://openws.work.weixin.qq.com` - `callback`: starts an HTTP server to receive message callbacks

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

bot_id.description: Bot ID obtained from the WeCom admin console.

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

encoding_aes_key_env.description: Env var name holding the EncodingAESKey (callback mode only).

secret_env.description: Env var name holding the bot secret.

token_env.description: Env var name holding the callback verification token (callback mode only).

webhook_port.description: Port for the callback HTTP server (only used in callback mode).

WeComMode.description: Connection mode for the WeCom intelligent bot adapter.

oneOf.description: URL callback (requires a publicly reachable HTTP endpoint).

timeout_secs.description: HTTP timeout for all web search requests (seconds). Recommended: 15 for most providers, 30+ for Jina.

max_chars.description: Maximum characters to return in content.

max_file_bytes.description: Maximum bytes a single `web_fetch_to_file` download may write to disk. Caps response size before the body reaches the workspace; an agent-supplied `max_bytes` parameter is further clamped down to this value, never up.

max_response_bytes.description: Maximum response body size in bytes.

ssrf_allowed_hosts.description: Hosts/CIDRs that are exempt from SSRF blocking (e.g. internal services in K8s). Cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.x.x, etc.) remain blocked unconditionally.

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

allowed_rooms.description: Room IDs to listen in (empty = all).

bot_token_env.description: Env var name holding the bot token.

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

account_id.description: Unique identifier for this bot instance (used for multi-bot routing).

callback_url.description: URL to POST outgoing messages to.

default_agent.description: Default agent name to route messages to.

deliver.description: Target channel name for direct delivery (e.g. "telegram", "discord"). Required when `deliver_only` is true.

deliver_only.description: When true, incoming POST bodies are forwarded directly to the delivery target channel without invoking the LLM or any agent. Requires `deliver` to be set to a valid channel name (not "log").

listen_port.description: Port to listen for incoming webhooks.

secret_env.description: Env var name holding the HMAC signing secret.

WebhookTriggerConfig.description:
Webhook trigger authentication configuration.

Controls the `/hooks/wake` and `/hooks/agent` endpoints for external systems to trigger agent actions.

enabled.description: Enable webhook trigger endpoints. Default: false.

max_payload_bytes.description: Max payload size in bytes. Default: 65536.

rate_limit_per_minute.description: Rate limit: max requests per minute per IP. Default: 30.

token_env.description: Env var name holding the bearer token (NOT the token itself). MUST be set if enabled=true. Token must be >= 32 chars.
